text.title: Spring Boot Code Examples and Antipatterns
text.summary: Concrete code examples for entity, repository, service, controller, mapper, and common antipatterns to reject.

example.note: UUID id, @Version, @CreationTimestamp, @UpdateTimestamp live in BaseEntity. All associations use LAZY fetch.
Class-level readOnly = true; write methods override with @Transactional. Service never returns entities.

antipattern.description: Anemic domain model: entity has only getters/setters, business logic lives in service.
antipattern.reason: Domain invariants (e.g., "can't promote if already admin") cannot be enforced; any service can bypass them.

antipattern.description: N+1 query: iterating a list and accessing a lazy association inside the loop.
antipattern.reason: Produces one query per item. Fix: JOIN FETCH in @Query or @EntityGraph on the repository method.

antipattern.description: @Transactional on a controller method or on a private service method.
antipattern.reason: Spring AOP proxy cannot intercept private methods or the controller layer; the annotation is silently ignored.
